nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: using AI to assist in drafting any kind of template

nodes.label: AI reliability in citations
nodes.grounding_span: I do not trust AI in its current state to provide accurate legal citations in any capacity

nodes.label: AI as initial draft generator
nodes.grounding_span: put that back into the AI to TEST it with specific details from hypotheticals

nodes.label: AI adherence to user's task framing
nodes.label_negative: AI unsolicited modification of user's work
nodes.grounding_span: the AI misunderstands the task and tries to rewrite or suggest modifications

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs delegator
nodes.label_negative: AI full delegation without iterative refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I will open up one tab and have the AI give me a full model policy... but I will also use a different tab to repeat the same task but with the granularity of the collaborative approach

nodes.label: AI as creative UI generator
nodes.label_negative: AI lack of creativity or simplistic outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I am always impressed when I ask the AI to create a fictional case study, and it actually produces something that is genuinely impressively creative

nodes.label: AI as skill gap filler
nodes.grounding_span: I am less and less concerned about an AI 'takeover' and more worried about an AI usage skills gap

nodes.label: Professional accountability
nodes.grounding_span: I want to be able to truthfully say--potentially under oath--that I did my due diligence and manually researched and added the relevant legal citations

nodes.label: Professional document quality and customer satisfaction
Control and guidance in task execution
nodes.grounding_span: I can go section by section and fine tune OR zoom out and change the whole document

nodes.label: Continuous learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I actively look for ways to work AI into my process so that I gain experience and understanding

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI for legal research
nodes.grounding_span: I do not trust AI in its current state to provide accurate legal citations in any capacity

nodes.label: Resistance to AI in creative work
nodes.grounding_span: I am always impressed when I ask the AI to create a fictional case study

nodes.label: Proactive concern about AI skills gap
nodes.grounding_span: I am less and less concerned about an AI 'takeover' and more worried about an AI usage skills gap

nodes.label: Pragmatic openness to expanding AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I actively look for ways to work AI into my process so that I gain experience and understanding

nodes.label: Backwards design approach — starting from final output
nodes.grounding_span: I generally use a backwards design approach; considering how the product will physically need to appear when it is complete and filled

nodes.label: Dual-tab cross-referencing — comparing delegation vs. collaboration
